company.bs: Eleanor Voss is the award-winning author of three novels exploring inherited memory and diaspora. Her latest work, The Salt Archive, was a finalist for the National Book Award and has been translated into twelve languages.

company.catchPhrase: Noir prose for an unquiet city.
company.bs: Marcus Whitfield writes character-driven crime fiction set in the Pacific Northwest. A former investigative journalist, he brings a documentarian's eye for detail to every page of his Detective Reyna series.

company.catchPhrase: Essays that sit with the uncomfortable.
company.bs: Priya Anand is an essayist and cultural critic whose collection Half-Light examines belonging, technology, and grief. Her work has appeared in The Atlantic and Granta.

company.catchPhrase: Speculative fiction with a human pulse.
company.bs: Julian Cortes writes near-future speculative fiction grounded in working-class life. His debut novel, Low Orbit, won the Crawford Award and was praised for its emotional precision.
